summary: Joel announces the release of CityDesk Service Pack 1, a free update shipped three months after the original version. The update fixes approximately 100 bugs and adds frequently requested features. The biggest user complaint was about weird URL generation, which surprised Joel since other CMS tools had the same issue. He highlights their crash reporting system as instrumental in discovering bugs.
key_points: CityDesk SP1 fixed about 100 bugs and added new features as a free service pack release, shipped three months after launch
The most common user complaint was about weird URL names, which Joel found surprising since competing CMS products had the same issue
Built-in crash reporting that automatically transmits crash info to the team proved valuable for finding and fixing bugs
Joel emphasizes shipping a few easy features that he believes will have outsized impact
money_quotes: The biggest complaint about CityDesk was that it generated weird URL names (which actually surprised me, because most other content management systems do the same).
We added a few easy features that I think will go a long way.
And we fixed all known bugs — many of which we found out about because CityDesk catches most crashes and transmits crucial crash info to us.
key_insight: Listening to user complaints and fixing them quickly — even when competitors have the same problems — is how you differentiate a product.
